verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation, facing the cytoplasm — not accessible from outside in its baseline state. However, ecto-SRC / cell-surface SRC activity has been documented on tumor cells and during certain activation states, consistent with cancer-associated outer-leaflet display of nominally intracellular kinases. This qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility. Not stable_complex_partner (no co-trafficked TM partner); not dual_localization in the cycling sense; not classical surface receptor.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Evidence for tumor ecto-SRC outer-leaflet display is limited; baseline biology is firmly inner-leaflet cytoplasmic-facing, so contextual call rests on cancer ecto-kinase precedent.
